text: So, in that case, we would have a binary situation where we would have positive or negative. I'll try to find dataset with similar underlying structure. That's underlying structure. And use that as a training data. And then I'll basically use a classic classifier. Usually, the baseline for me is to use stuff like ResNet and other stuff like that. If we're talking about images for stuff like classifying, we could see the baseline fine tune models or ready made models. Use one of them to classify whether the customer reviews positive or negative and training it on the data I found. And then, with the including the imbalanced data, the only way to handle imbalanced data is to augment the smaller portion of the less representative portion of the data. And the way to evaluate is just basically mean squared error to see.
